academic_requirements.summary: Applicants need a minimum three-year bachelor's degree in Psychology or equivalent, to be obtained by July 31, 2026, with proven competencies in General Psychology, Psychobiology and Physiological Psychology, and Psychometrics, plus a GPA of at least 85% of the maximum obtainable grade.
academic_requirements.prior_degree_or_diploma: A minimum three-year undergraduate degree (or equivalent) in Psychology.
academic_requirements.subject_requirements: Applicants must have proven competencies in General Psychology, Psychobiology and Physiological Psychology, and Psychometrics.
academic_requirements.gpa_or_country_requirements: A GPA of at least 85% of the maximum obtainable grade is required. Applicants must also satisfy the University of Padua country-specific entry-title requirements.

language_requirements.required_level: English B2 (CEFR) or equivalent, to be achieved by July 31, 2026.
accepted_proofs: IELTS Academic or General Training with minimum score 6.0
Cambridge English B2 First with minimum score 173
Oxford Test of English with minimum score 126
Pearson PTE Academic with minimum score 65
Other certificates explicitly accepted on the official University of Padua language requirement page
exemption_rules: Applicants are exempt if the previous degree used as the entry qualification was completed entirely in English.
Applicants are exempt if they are native English speakers from one of the countries listed by the University of Padua.
Only certificates obtained no more than five years before the start date of the selection process are accepted.

application_deadline_eu.notes: This call is for EU and EU-equated candidates with a foreign qualification; eligible non-EU candidates already residing in Italy also follow this route.

application_deadline_non_eu.notes: This call is for non-EU candidates residing abroad. The official non-EU call lists 20 available places.

required_documents.notes: The EU and EU-equated call accepts a valid identity document or passport; the non-EU abroad call explicitly requires a passport.

required_documents.document_name: Academic transcript of records and bachelor's degree diploma
required_documents.notes: Upload the transcript with exams, grades, and grading scale, and the bachelor's diploma if already obtained.

required_documents.document_name: Official translations into Italian or English
required_documents.notes: Required if academic documents are issued in a language other than Italian or English.
Upload if already obtained; if missing at application stage it must be achieved and submitted by July 31, 2026.
A programme-specific evaluation form using the official Annex 1 template is required.
Required as part of the online application.

required_documents.document_name: Legalisation or Apostille or CIMEA Statement of Verification
required_documents.notes: Admitted candidates must submit one of these qualification-verification documents by October 31, 2026. A CIMEA Statement of Comparability may also be requested if needed.

entry_exam_or_test.test_name: No fixed written entry exam; ranked evaluation with possible online interview
entry_exam_or_test.dates: No scheduled written test is stated. The committee may conduct an online interview to assess competencies if needed during the admission evaluation.
entry_exam_or_test.scoring_or_threshold: Ranking is based on a 100-point scale: GPA up to 30 points, congruence of prior studies up to 40 points, and relevant activities such as work experience, internships, research, mobility, or publications up to 30 points. Candidates must reach at least 60/100 to enter the ranking.

source_quotes.quote: A GPA of at least 85% of the maximum obtainable grade

uncertainty_notes: The University of Padua states that English translations of admission documents are for information only; in case of disputes, the Italian version has legal validity.
The official 2026/2027 call PDFs for this programme are stored in a `/2025/` website directory, but the document text clearly states the 2026/2027 application periods and requirements.
